title: Pre-Determined and Re-Adjusted Stances — empty self-philosophy template
html:
TL;DR

  • Feb 1, 2023 template doc: an empty manifesto skeleton Yahir titled "Rules that govern your thinking" — 19 unanswered category headers (Dad, Mom, Sister, Brother, Sexuality, Girls, Love, Computer Science, Anime, Politics, Mexican-American, Idols, Meta-Thinking, Slave to responsibility, Future Career, Entrepreneurship, Hobbies, Depression, Book/Reading, Life).
  • Zero filled-in content — every header has a colon and an empty line. Pure intent-without-execution artifact.
  • Notable for what's on the list: Sexuality + Girls + Love (three separate buckets — the questioning-self frame from his 2023 recap "questioning who I am... in sexuality"); Mexican-American as identity bucket; Anime as standalone; Slave-to-Responsibility self-named.
  • Sits in the same Q1-2023 self-philosophy push as excessively net positive person and Singularity / validation from within.
  • Useful as negative-space evidence: Yahir was actively trying to codify his stances but couldn't finish — the manifesto style impulse meets the paralysis.

Notes

This is a manifesto style note that didn't ship — but the categories themselves are the data. Compare to actual filled-in self-philosophy artifacts like year recap and Té Amo critique: the empty headers reveal the dimensions Yahir thought needed his own POV, even when he couldn't articulate one yet. "Slave to responsibility" naming itself is meta-aware — same self-diagnosis that becomes older brother as anchor / burn bright later.

The Sexuality/Girls/Love trichotomy maps to the emily arc / Jo / Sam question-cluster from 2023.

Connections

  • — the genre attempted
  • fear of not doing — the paralysis explanation
  • older brother as anchor · burn bright die young — the "slave to responsibility" self-diagnosis
  • 2024 01 01 2023 year recap — the actually-completed reflection from same year
  • piercing prompts — what a non-empty version would look like
